Question: A giromatic hand piece for endodontic instrumentation operates by a:
Explanation:
[Grossman 12th ed 230]
Giromatic and Racer, both are contra-angle engine driven instruments. Giromatic hand piece uses a barbed broach or reamer through 90Â° reciprocating arc at a speed of 1000 cycles/min. The racer hand piece uses a standard file and oscillates the file in root canal. These instruments can be used for opening root canals but should not be used for canal preparation because root canals prepared by hand instruments are superior in shape and smoothness.

Explanation:
[Grossman 12th ed 228]
File contains more number of flutes and is a four sided instrument because of which it is highly resistant to torque. The file contains more metal and is least likely to break in a root canal. The barbed broach is used for extirpation of pulp and most likely to break in a root canal WHEN TWISTED MORE than 90Â° (PGI-95).

|---|---|

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Difference between physical characteristic of reamers and files is:
Option A: The cross-section of reamers in square and files are triangular in cross-section
Option B: The number of flutes on the blade are more in files than in reamers
Option C: The reamers have more flutes in the blade
Option D: Files have two superficial grooves to produce flutes in a double helix design
Explanation:
[Grossman 12th ed 228]
| Conventional file | Square blank | Less cutting efficiency than reamer |
|---|---|---|
| K-Flex file | Rhomboidal/ diamond shaped with alternating high and low flutes | High flexibility and cutting efficiency |
| Headstroem or H-Files | Made from round blank and contains spiral flutes | Higher cutting efficiency and more prone to fracture |

Option C: Inflexible and used at low speed
Option D: Inflexible and used at high speed
Explanation:
[Grossman 12th ed 232]
Gates Glidden drill and Peeso reamer are the two types of power driven instruments.
Gates-Glidden drill is used:
â¢ to remove lingual shoulder (PGI-96) during access cavity preparation in anterior teeth.
â¢ to create radicular access by enlarging root canal orifices.
â¢ for shaping cervical third of R.C in step-back preparation.
Peso reamer is used for post space preparation. Both Gates Glidden drill and peso reamer are aggressive cutting instruments and inflexible and should be used at low speed with extreme caution.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Which of the following is a disinfectant, sterilizing agent and an antiseptic?
Explanation:
[Grossman 12th ed 226]
Sodium hypochlorite is most effective among five halogen antiseptics. It acts as
â¢ lubricant during instrumentation in root canal.
â¢ solvent for dissolution of pulp
â¢ antiseptic and disinfectant by combining both protoplasm of bacterial cell and destroying it.
â¢ bleaching agent by releasing nascent oxygen.
NaOCl has been reported to cause dentine discolouration, although it is a bleaching agent. This discolouration is a result of its contact with erythrocytes and its high tendency to crystallize on the root dentine, which may mean that it is difficult to completely remove from the canal. In addition, the combination of NaOCl with other adjunct irrigating solutions has been found to cause marked tooth discolourations.
False about sodium hypochlorite? (AIPG-2015)
a) Chelation
b) Antimicrobial effect
c) Tissue dissolving property
d) Can stain dentin
Ans: a

The sonic instruments operate at 1500 - 6500 cycles/ min (25-110/sec.). Ultrasonic system uses sodium hypochlorite as irrigant, whereas the sonic system uses water as an irrigant.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Resistance form of endodontics is:
Option A: Resists movement of gutta-percha in apical area
Option B: To allow use of spreader in lateral condensation
Option C: Fracture of root while vertical condensation
Explanation:
[Ingle 5th ed 432]
The principles of root canal preparation are:
i) Outline form
ii) Convenience form
iii) Toilet of the cavity
iv) Retention form
v) Resistance form
vi) Extension for prevention
i) Outline form:
â¢ Must be correctly shaped to establish complete access for instrumentation from cavity margin to apex.
â¢ At the coronal margin of the cavity, the outline form must be continually evaluated by monitoring the tension of the endodontic instruments against the margin of the cavity.
ii) Convenience form:
â¢ The access should be expanded so that the instruments do not bind, especially when larger and less flexible instruments are used.
iii) Toilet of cavity:
â¢ Success depends to a great extent on whether unreachable debris laden with viable bacteria with a source of substrate (accessory canal or microleakage) to survive. Hence the importance of thorough douching through irrigation, toilet of the cavity.
iv) Retention form:
â¢ It is recommended that the master gutta-percha cone should fit tightly in the apical 2 to 3mm of the canal.
â¢ These nearly parallel walls (retention form) ensure the form seating of this principle point.
â¢ These final 2 to 3mm of cavity are the most crucial for meticulous care in preparation. This is where the sealing against future leakage or percolation into the canal takes place. This is also the region where accessory or lateral canals are most apt to be present.
v) Resistance form:
â¢ Resistance to overfilling is the primary objective of resistance form. This can be achieved by terminating the canal preparation at the dentinocemental junction which is approximately 0.5mm from the outer surface of the root.
â¢ Beyond this point, we are dealing with the tissues of the periodontal ligament space, but not the pulp.
â¢ The tiny remaining portion of the canal (beyond DCJ) is not properly cleaned and may contain bacteria and packed debris. This section of the canal should be cleaned but not shaped with fine instruments such as No.10 or 15 files. This action is known as establishing apical patency.
vi) Extension for prevention:
â¢ The extension of the cavity from the crown to apex is necessary to ensure prevention of future problems.
â¢ Peripheral enlargement of the canal, to remove all of the debris, followed by obturation is the primary preventive method.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The last solution used in root canal treatment to avoid painful surgical emphysema before dressing is
Explanation:
[Grossman 11th ed 189]
Alternate irrigations with sodium hypochlorite and hydrogen peroxide have been advocated in root canal preparation. The advantages of alternating solutions of 3% hydrogen peroxide and 5.2% sodium hypochlorite are-
â¢ The effervescent reaction, in which it mechanically "bubbles" and pushes debris out of the root canal.
â¢ The solvent action of the sodium hypochlorite on the organic debris of the pulp tissue.
â¢ The disinfecting and bleaching action by both solutions.
Finally, sodium chlorite should be used before dressing because hydrogen peroxide can react with pulp debris and blood to form gas (surgical emphysema) that will cause continuous tooth pain.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The superelastic property of the NiTi wire is due to
Option A: Stress induced phase transition from the austenitic to the martensitic phase
Option B: Temperature induced phase transition from the martensitic to the austenitic phase
Option C: Stress induced phase transition from the martensitic to the austenitic phase
Option D: Temperature induced phase transition from the austenitic to the martensitic phase
Explanation:
[Phillips 1st SA ed 435/ Proffit 5th ed 363]
Austenitic NiTi is the high temperature, low stress form while martensitic NiTi is the low temperature high stress form. Transformation between austentic and martensitic forms of NiTi can be induced by both temperature and stress. The uniqueness of NiTi is that transition between the two structures is fully reversible and occurs at a remarkably low temperature. This phase transition allows NiTi to exhibit two properties found in no other dental materials-shape memory and super elasticity.
Shape Memory (Thermal reaction) refers to the ability of the material to remember its original shape after being plastically deformed while still in the martensitic font. This is temperature induced change.
Cooling causes plastic deformation -> Austenitic -> Martensitic -> After Heating changed to original shape
Super elasticity (mechanical reaction) refers to the very large reversible strains that NiTi wire can withstand due to the martensitic-austenite phase transition. Materials displaying super elasticity are austenite alloys that undergo transition to martensite in response to stress. It is a mechanical (stress) analogue to the thermally induced shape memory effect.
Austenite -> Stress -> Super elasticity -> Martensite

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Which file is used along with irrigation for ultrasonic system?
Explanation:
[Cohen 10th ed 256]
After the root canal is shaped, a small file (size 15) is introduced as far apically as possible in the root canal filled with irrigant and ultrasonically activated. This No.15 size file or wire moves freely and the irrigant can penetrate more easily into the apical part of the canal. Files of sizes 20, 25 may produce less accoustic streaming. Their use is restricted to very wide canals.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: In Endodontics Endotec is used
Option A: with lateral condensation and heated gutta percha
Explanation:
[Ingle 6th ed 1062]
Cold or lateral compaction is easy and quick to perform while superior density is gained through vertical compaction of warm guttapercha. Martin developed a device that appears to achieve the best qualities of both techniques called Endotec II. He claims that Endotec combines the best of the two most popular obturation techniques, warm/ vertical and the relatively simplicity of lateral compaction. It contains battery powered, heat controlled spreader/plugger that ensures complete thermo-softening of any type of guttapercha.
Endotec II is? (PGI June-12)
a) Obturation system
b) Irrigating device
c) Biomechanical preparation
d) Antibiotic
Ans: a

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Step down technique is
Option A: Preparation from apical to middle third
Option B: Preparation from apical to coronal third
Option C: Preparation from coronal to middle third
Option D: Preparation from coronal to apical third
Explanation:
[Ingle 5th ed 470, Grossman 12th ed 258]
Step-back: Beginning the preparation at the apex and working back up the canal coronally with larger and larger instruments.
Step-down: Often called "Crown-down" approach, begins coronally and the preparation is advanced apically, using smaller and smaller instruments, finally terminating at the apical stop.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Instrument having Shank with alternating cutting edges
Explanation:
[Cohen 9th ed 312, Grossman 12th ed 235]
RACE (Reamer with alternating cutting edges)
â¢ Light microscopic imaging of the file shows twisted areas (a feature of conventional files) alternating with straight areas.
â¢ Cross sections are triangular or square for # 0.02 instruments with size # 15 and # 20 tips.
â¢ The lengths of cutting parts vary from 9-16mm.
â¢ Alternating cutting edges eliminate the screwing into the dentin.
â¢ Sharp cutting efficiency, combination of triangular section and alternating cutting edges ensures efficient evacuation of debris and safety tip ensures guidance/ centring in the canal.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: In Ingle's method of Endodontic working length determination, safety allowance is for
Explanation:
[Ingle 6th ed 929]
Safety allowance (done before working length x-ray) is the substraction of 1mm length from the diagnostic radiograph before taking the working length. This is for the possible image distortion or magnification in the diagnostic film. Safety allowance factor (done after WL x-ray) is adjusting working length 0.5-1mm to confirm the length to apical constriction

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The potentially hazardous irrigant causing an emergency during treatment is
Explanation:
[Grossman 12th ed 493]
Extrusion of sodium hypochlorine into periradicular tissues causes the following effects due to its tissue dissolving effect.
a) Immediate effects:
â¢ Swelling
â¢ Pain
â¢ Ecchymosis
b) Long term effects:
â¢ Parasthesia
â¢ Scarring
â¢ Muscle weakness
â¢ Extrusion of H2O2 causing tissue emphysema.
Treatment includes antibiotics, analgesics, intramuscular steriods and surgical intervention with wound debridement.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The file which has a non-cutting side is
Explanation:
[Grossman 12th ed 230]
Modifications of H-files:
i) Safety hed stroem: It is H-file with a non cutting side in order to prevent ledging in curved canals.
ii) Hyflex file: It has 'S' shape cross section instead of single helix tear drop cross section of the H-file.
iii) Unifiles: It has double helix cross section. These are no longer used.
iv) S-file: It is a variation of unifile

Explanation:
[Grossman 12th ed 231]
Ultrasonic instrumentation never fulfilled clinical expectations as a primary method to prepare root canals. They were never predictable, effective of efficient as the conventional methods.
But ultrasonics are promising for:
â¢ Activating irrigants
â¢ Removal of smear layer
â¢ Removal of posts and silver points

Explanation:

Explanation:

The profile GT set consists of 4 instruments, having tip size of 20, with taper 0.06, 0.08, 0.10 and 0.12 mm/ mm. 0.12 mm/mm taper is also available in sizes No. 35, No. 50 and No. 70.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: In K-file the most common type of motion is
Explanation:
[Grossman 12th ed 251]
Files are predominantly used with filling or rasping or push or pull action (linear) in which there is little or no rotation in the root canals.
â¢ Reaming: Motion involving clockwise rotation of instrument
â¢ Watch winding: Back and forth oscillation right and left. The angle of rotation is 30-60Â°C
â¢ Watch winding and pull: motion can be used with both file and reamer. But most common type of motion of K-file is push and pull (linear) motion. Used with H-files.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: File commonly used for flaring of the canal in case of retreatment:
Explanation:
[Cohen 8th ed 528, 888]
H-files remove large amount of dentin. The gutta-percha in the canal can be heated and H-files can be used to remove it in case of retreatment.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: In NiTi rotary electric handpiece, rotation should be
Explanation:
[Grossman 12th ed 234]
The rotational speeds for various Ni-Ti instruments fall commonly under 150-300 RPM. Light speed rotary instrumentation system requires 750-2000 RPM.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: In order to complete a cutting circle of canal wall, the triangular shaft instrument requires:
Explanation:
[Practical dentistry by Balwant Rai Pg 17]
The cross sectional shape of the shaft of the instrument is important. The triangular shaft requires a one-third (120 degrees) rotation of the instrument to complete a cutting circle of the root canal wall. Square shaft required a one-fourth turn (90 degrees) to do the same task.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The significance of free eugenol in root canal sealar cements is an increase in:
Explanation:
[Ingle 6th ed 1039/Cohen 10th ed 264]
Free eugenol always remains the mass of zinc oxide eugenol sealers. This contributes to cytotoxicity and irritation to the tissues if the free eugenol content increases in the mass.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The objectives of cleaning and shaping are all the following EXCEPT
Option B: To shape/contour the root canal walls
Option C: To aid condensation of an inert filling material
Option D: Should keep the apical foramen as small as practical
Explanation:
[Grossman 12th ed 221, Nisha Garg 2nd ed 232]
Schilder objectives of cleaning and shaping of root canals:
a) Mechanical objectives:
â¢ Continuous tapering canal shape, with the narrowest cross-sectional diameter apically and the widest diameter coronally.
â¢ Walls should taper evenly towards apex
â¢ To give the prepared root canal the quality of flow.
â¢ Should keep the apical foramen as small as practical.
b) Biologic objectives:
â¢ To debride and disinfect root canal system
â¢ Necrotic debris should not be forced periapically
â¢ Sufficient space for intracanal medicaments and irrigants should be created.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: The commonly used root canal irrigant, which was used for irrigation wounds during world war 1 by Dakin is
Explanation:
[Ingle 6th ed 998]
Sodium hypochlorite:
â¢ It is used in concentrations varying from 0.5 to 7%
â¢ 2.5% and 5% are most commonly used.
â¢ Very potent antimicrobial agent
â¢ Effectively dissolves pulpal remnants
â¢ 0.5% Naocl which is buffered with a bicarbonate buffer is called Dakins solution. Unbuffered Naocl has a pH of 11, while buffered form has pH of 9
Disadvantages:
â¢ Inability to remove smear layer
â¢ Unpleasant taste
â¢ Toxicity

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Cleaning of files in between of endodontic treatment is done by:
Explanation:
[Grossman 11th ed 196]
During instrumentation in the root canal, debris is often collected on the instruments. This debris can clog the flutes of the instruments and decrease their efficiency. This debris can be removed by squeezing the blade between the layers of gauze and turning the instrument counter clockwise.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: In root canal treatment, the canal orifices are located by:
Explanation:
[Cohen 10 ed 156/ Grossman 12th ed 240]
Endodontic explorers have a long tip with a sharp point that is used to locate the opening of small canals (pathfinder) during endodontic procedures. They are made of spring tempered stainless steel so they retain their shape and resist breakage. Both tips of DG16 endodontic explorers are 16mm in length and set at different angles 45Â° and 70Â°. Jw 17 serves the same purpose but its thinner stiffer tip can be used for identifying calcified canals. A # 17 operative explorer is useful for detecting any remaining pulp chamber roof, particularly in the area of a pulp horn.

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Advaritage of NiTi over K-files?
Explanation:
[Nisha Garg 3rd ed 157]
The shape memory and superelasticity contribute to the following advantages of NiTi over stainless steel.
Advantages:
â¢ More flexibility
â¢ Able to conform to canal curvature
â¢ Low modulus of elasticity
â¢ Good resiliency
â¢ Corrosion resistance
â¢ Softer than stainless steel
Disadvantages:
â¢ Poor cutting efficiency
â¢ Dot no show signs of deformation before they fracture
â¢ Less fracture resistance

Explanation:
[Grossman 12th ed 284]

Subject: Endodontics
Question: Instruments used for removal of lingual shoulder and enlarge orifices of a root canal are
Explanation:
[Grossman 12th ed 232]
GG burs are used to remove the lingual shoulder in anterior teeth and enlarging the orifices of root canal. They provide straight light access to the initial point of curvature of root canal.
